𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Nine — Part Nine

The Book of Tehillim

TEHILLIM

The Psalms — The Praises of Yisra'EL

Psalms 151, 154, and 155

DSS Additional Psalms — Physically Confirmed at Qumran — 11QPsa Cave 11

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

11Q5 (11QPsa) — Cave 11 — Major Psalms Scroll — sole Hebrew source for all three

Syriac Peshitta — Psalms 154 and 155 — secondary witness

Septuagint — Psalm 151 only — Greek translation — secondary witness

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete — all three psalms preserved in Beta Israel canon

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

DSS ADDITIONAL PSALMS — NOT IN THE MASORETIC CANON — PHYSICALLY CONFIRMED AT QUMRAN

These three psalms were physically discovered in Cave 11 at Qumran in 1956 as part of the Great Psalms Scroll — 11QPsa (11Q5). They appear in the scroll after Psalm 150 as part of the Qumran community's Psalter. They are not found in the Masoretic canon but are preserved in the Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez, the Greek Septuagint (Psalm 151 only), and the Syriac Peshitta (Psalms 154 and 155). Their original Hebrew composition is confirmed by the Cave 11 manuscript. This compilation follows the DSS Cave 11 Hebrew text as the primary authority over all secondary witnesses.

TEHILLIM — PSALM 151 Psalm 151 — Of Dawid — A Hallelujah — Physically Confirmed at Qumran — I Was Small Among My Brothers

1 I was small among my brothers and the youngest in my father's house — I tended my father's flocks.

2 My hands made a flute and my fingers a lyre — I rendered glory to YHWH.

3 I said to myself: The mountains do not witness to him, nor do the hills proclaim — but the trees have cherished my words and the flock my deeds.

4 For who can proclaim and who can speak and who can recount the deeds of YHWH? Creator saw all — he heard everything and he heeded.

5 He sent his prophet to anoint me — Shemu'el to make me great — my brothers went out to meet him, handsome of figure and appearance.

6 Though they were tall of stature and handsome by their hair, YHWH Creator chose them not.

7 He sent and took me from behind the flock and anointed me with holy oil — and he made me leader of his people and ruler over the sons of his covenant.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] Psalm 151 is physically attested in 11QPsa (11Q5) from Cave 11 at Qumran — found in 1956. The Cave 11 Psalms Scroll contains this psalm immediately after Psalm 150, confirming its place in the Qumran Psalter. It is also preserved in the Greek Septuagint and in the Orit Ge'ez. The Cave 11 scroll preserves a longer Hebrew version than the Septuagint, with the full seven-verse text given here. The DSS Hebrew is the primary text.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Psalm 151 in full as a canonical psalm of the Ethiopian Israelite tradition. Psalm 151 is accepted as scripture in the Beta Israel canon. The Orit Ge'ez and the DSS Cave 11 text are in substantial agreement.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] Psalm 151 is not part of the Masoretic canon and does not appear in the received Hebrew Bible tradition. It is known in the Masoretic tradition only through the Greek Septuagint where it appears as an appendix marked as outside the number. The DSS Cave 11 Hebrew text confirms its antiquity and its original Hebrew composition. This compilation follows the DSS Cave 11 reading as the primary authority.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Psalm 151 is the self-testimony of Dawid — his account of his own calling. The contrast with 1 Shemu'el 16 is revealing: the canonical account focuses on Shemu'el seeing YHWH's choice fall on Dawid. Psalm 151 focuses on Dawid's own interior experience — he was small, he was forgotten, he tended flocks and made music. He rendered glory to YHWH through his flute and lyre before he was ever anointed. Verse 7 — he sent and took me from behind the flock and anointed me with holy oil — and he made me leader of his people and ruler over the sons of his covenant. The anointing is YHWH's act alone. The smallness that preceded it is not erased — it is the condition YHWH chose.

TEHILLIM — PSALM 154 Psalm 154 — A Psalm of Hezekiah — Praise YHWH With Understanding — Proclaim His Glory in the Assembly

1 With a loud voice glorify Creator — in the congregation of the many proclaim his majesty.

2 Among the multitude of the upright glorify his name and with the faithful recount his greatness.

3 Join your souls to the good and to the perfect to glorify the Most High.

4 Form an assembly to proclaim his salvation and be not slack in making known his might and his majesty to all simple folk.

5 For to make known the glory of YHWH is wisdom given — and for recounting his many deeds she is revealed to man.

6 To make known to the simple his might — to explain to the senseless his greatness — those far from her gates, those who stray from her portals.

7 For the Most High is YHWH of Yaakov and his majesty is over all his works.

8 And a man who glorifies the Most High he accepts as one who brings a meal offering — as one who offers he-goats and bullocks — as one who fattens the altar with many burnt offerings — as a sweet savor from the hand of the righteous.

9 From the gates of the righteous is heard her voice and from the assembly of the pious her song.

10 When they eat with satiety she is cited and when they drink in community together.

11 Their meditation is on the Torah of the Most High, her words they proclaim in the night watches.

12 For as a crown of glory is she to them and a scepter of honor.

13 Never has tree been planted like her in the garden of Eden — fruit-laden and stately.

14 Nor have pomegranates blossomed in Eden like her blossoms — nor has the planting of cypress mixed with her.

15 For her light is like the light of fire — her coals blaze and consume.

16 All who grasp her are given life — but they that forsake her are delivered to death.

17 YHWH the Creator of all who created her knows her — there is none who can search out his thought.

18 For he established her before the world — and forever shall she endure.

19 He has given her to his chosen ones and they shall inherit her forever.

20 Wisdom shall not be silent — nor shall she cease from declaring her praises.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] Psalm 154 is physically attested in 11QPsa (11Q5) from Cave 11 at Qumran — found in 1956 — and also in the Syriac Peshitta tradition. The Cave 11 Hebrew text is the primary authority. The psalm is a wisdom hymn presenting Wisdom personified as the voice of YHWH's instruction among the righteous assembly. It has no equivalent in the Masoretic canon.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Psalm 154 in the Beta Israel psalter tradition. The psalm's identification of Torah with personified Wisdom — whose light is like fire, who is a crown of glory, who was established before the world — is consistent with the Wisdom theology of the Ethiopian Israelite canon.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] Psalm 154 is not part of the Masoretic canon and has no equivalent in the received Hebrew Bible tradition. It is known only through the DSS Cave 11 Psalms Scroll and the Syriac Peshitta. This compilation follows the DSS Cave 11 Hebrew text as the primary authority.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Psalm 154 presents Wisdom — Torah personified — as the central figure of the covenant assembly's life. Verse 11 — their meditation is on the Torah of the Most High, her words they proclaim in the night watches. This is Psalm 1:2 and Psalm 119:148 embodied in a community: the covenant assembly meditates on Torah day and night, proclaiming her words in the night watches. Verse 13 — never has tree been planted like her in the garden of Eden. Wisdom is the tree of the garden — connecting the Torah directly to Bereshit 2. Verse 20 — wisdom shall not be silent. The closing declaration is that Wisdom — Torah — will continue to declare her praises forever. The covenant instruction does not cease.

TEHILLIM — PSALM 155 Psalm 155 — A Prayer of Deliverance — I Cried to You YHWH — Deliver My Soul From the Wicked

1 YHWH, I called to you — hear me — I spread forth my hands toward your holy dwelling.

2 Incline your ear and grant me my plea and turn not away your face from me in the day of my distress.

3 Teach me your statutes that I may not sin against you and not stumble in my ways.

4 Let not the ruler of the pit seize me nor the spirit of uncleanness have dominion over me.

5 For flesh and spirit are yours — you made them — but let not wanton iniquity rule over me.

6 For you are my praise, YHWH, and in you has my hope been all my days.

7 Shall the brother of my mother be judged by you? Let him not be despised before you — he did walk before you.

8 Remember that I am dust — am I not made of clay? — formed from water — fashioned by your hands.

9 Rescue me from the hand of the wicked — deliver my soul from the place of the wicked.

10 Let me not fall into the hand of a man more powerful than I — let me not be put to shame.

11 Your mercy, YHWH, is before me always — I have put my trust in your salvation.

12 Let not the accuser stand at my right hand — let not the adversary triumph over me.

13 Send righteousness from above like dew — like rain on the earth.

14 Turn away from my soul the pain of the wicked — let the heart of the proud be humbled.

15 Let the wicked be ashamed and confused — let them not see the light of day.

16 As for me — I have hoped in you, YHWH — I cried to you and you answered me.

17 For you showed me your righteousness and directed my steps in your paths.

18 You have established my feet on firm ground — I shall not be shaken by any adversary.

19 Blessed are you, YHWH my Creator — forever blessed be your holy name.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] Psalm 155 is physically attested in 11QPsa (11Q5) from Cave 11 at Qumran — found in 1956. The psalm is a personal lament and prayer of deliverance with strong affinities to Psalms 31, 51, and 141. It has no equivalent in the Masoretic canon. The Cave 11 Hebrew text is the primary authority.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Psalm 155 in the Beta Israel psalter tradition. The psalm's prayer against the ruler of the pit — verse 4 — and the spirit of uncleanness reflects the same angelological framework as the Chanokh literature and the War Scroll in the broader DSS corpus.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] Psalm 155 is not part of the Masoretic canon and has no equivalent in the received Hebrew Bible tradition. It is known only through the DSS Cave 11 Psalms Scroll. This compilation follows the DSS Cave 11 Hebrew text as the primary authority.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 4 — let not the ruler of the pit seize me nor the spirit of uncleanness have dominion over me. The ruler of the pit — the angel of Sheol — and the spirit of uncleanness are the same hostile spiritual forces described in the Community Rule, the War Scroll, and the Books of Chanokh. The prayer of Psalm 155 assumes the same cosmology as the rest of the DSS corpus: unseen adversarial forces are real, named, and subject to YHWH's authority. Verse 8 — remember that I am dust — am I not made of clay — formed from water — fashioned by your hands. The same covenant argument as Psalm 103:14 — he remembers that we are dust. The psalmist appeals to YHWH's own knowledge of what he made. Verse 19 — blessed are you, YHWH my Creator — forever blessed be your holy name. The DSS Additional Psalms close with blessing, as the canonical Psalms 1-150 close with praise.

END OF PART NINE — PSALMS 151, 154, 155 — DSS ADDITIONAL PSALMS COMPLETE

Volume Nine — Tehillim — Part Nine

𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

TEHILLIM — THE BOOK OF PSALMS — ALL 153 PSALMS — COMPLETE

Psalms 1-150 canonical — Psalms 151, 154, 155 DSS confirmed — 11QPsa Cave 11

Primary: Dead Sea Scrolls — Supporting: Orit Ge'ez — YHWH and The Creator only
